&Context_Example: DO NOT CHANGE:: This is an example context comment. Do not modify these. These will not appear in the actual game
language.name: English
Context_0: Mode as in Minecraft's survival mode, creative mode etc.
editor.title: Editor
&Context_1: A sector is a 2D polygonal room defined by multiple vertices
Context_2: Clicking on refresh texture looks at the user's files and updates the list of available texture
&Context_3: Floor as in the story of a building. First floor, second floor etc.
&Context_4: -Shutdown- is forcing the engine to instantly close without saving or safety checks
&Context_5: UI stands for User Interface. Clicking on this button opens up the UI Editor Screen
&Context_6: Exporting is turning the game into a stand-alone .exe
ui_editor.title: UI Editor
&Context_7: Master Gain is basically the absolute volume slider for the entire game.
&Context_8: Level as in a video game level. It consists of entities and rooms
levels.title: Levels
&Context_9: If the user is in wall mode, then the editor creates walls. If in sector mode, then the editor creates sectors etc.
sector.title: Sector
&Context_10: Height of the room's ceiling and floor. So if the floor has a height of 10 and ceiling 50, the room will take up 40 units of space. Starting from y = 10
&Context_11: As in the amount of floors in a building
&Context_12: As in the floor you walk on
wall.title: Wall
&Context_13: The sector that is to the north of the wall when looking from a bird's eye view
&Context_14: The sector that is to the sout of the wall when looking from a bird's eye view
&Context_15: As in the floors in a building. So if wall.floor = 2, this means the walls is on the second story
&Context_17: An entity, can also be called an object is basically anything in the game world aside from walls and sectors. Same as Unity's Gameobject
entity.name: Name
entity.title: Entity
&Context_18: A component is a (figurative) box of information that can be attached to an entity. For example a Transform Component would store information about the entity's position & rotation. And a Rigidbody Component would store information about the entity's velocity
&Context_19: Transform stores information about the entity's position, rotation and other whereabouts such as the current sector it is in
&Context_20: Sprite is a 2D billboarded image that always faces the camera, like enemies in Doom 1993
&Context_21: A decal is a sprite that is attached to a wall. Like a sticker
&Context_22: An audio source is a component that stores information about an audio file like its length and volume
&Context_23: 2D Cartesian position is defined by two coordinates (x, y)
&Context_24: Height is the distance from the floor. (Relative to the current sector the entity is in) So a height of 10 means the entity is flying 10 units above the floor
&Context_25: Floor as in the stories of a building. So a transform.floor = 2 means the entity is at the 2nd floor
&Context_26: The index of the texture in the array texture
&Context_27: The wall that the decal is attached to
&Context_28: The y position of the decal. decal.z_offset = 0 means the decal touches the floor while decal.z_offset = 10 means it is 10 units above the floor
&Context_29: Same thing but for the x axis. decal.wall_offset = 0 means the decal is at the most left of the wall
&Context_30: Distance from the wall in the z axis. If this is higher than 0 that means the decal will look like it flies midair
&Context_31: decal.abs_height is a boolean that if true, the decal's y position will not change even if the wall it is attached to moves up or down
&Context_32: A script is a piece of code the user can write to add custom functionality
&Context_33: A player controller is a component that has built-in functionalities for player movement.
&Context_34: Y position of the camera relative to the floor's height
&Context_35: The maximum step height the player can go up to. As in stairs
&Context_38: If no clip is on, the player will not collide with anything
&Context_40: If the distance of an object to the camera is smaller than camera.near_plane, it will not render
&Context_41: If the distance of an object to the camera is larger than camera.far_plane, it will not render
&Context_42: Is trigger means the collider will register collision detection, but will not do collision resolution
&Context_43: Short for: Axis Aligned Bounding Box
&Context_45: Length, width and height of the AABB box as in (x, y, z)
&Context_46: A rigidbody is a component that stores information about the physics-related part of the entity. Such as its velocity & mass
&Context_47: If rigidbody.is_static = true then the object will not move
&Context_48: A UI transform is a component that defines the position, size, and alignment of an entity within a user interface layout, relative to its parent container.
&Context_49: The normalized position in the parent UI element that the bottom-left corner of this UI element anchors to (from 0.0 to 1.0).
&Context_50: The normalized position in the parent UI element that the top-right corner of this UI element anchors to (from 0.0 to 1.0).
&Context_51: The local point around which the UI element rotates, scales, and positions itself, defined in normalized coordinates (0,0 is bottom-left, 1,1 is top-right).
&Context_52: Name of the component -Text- that stores a string to be displayed on the screen
&Context_55: -Tilky- (Specialized way to spell the Turkish world -Tilki_ which means fox) is a special name that you do not need to translate, However, you can phonetically transcribe or transliterate it if you wish so
launcher.name: Tilky Engine Launcher
